question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicController calling topicService.findById(), entityService.findById(), and customFeeService.findById() independently without atomicity, causing a TOCTOU race where the custom fee schedule changes between the topicService call and the customFeeService call, returning a mixed/inconsistent response that causes fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter regex allowing up to 12 digits for num ('^...(\d{1,12})$'), supply a 12-digit num that when encoded via EntityId.of(shard, realm, num) produces a long that collides with a different topic's primary key in the database, causing findById() to return wrong fee data and leading to fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl is declared 'final' and package-private, but TopicService interface is public, allowing a malicious Spring bean registered at runtime to replace the TopicService bean and return crafted Topic objects with manipulated custom fee data, causing fund loss for all users of the API?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of response caching validation in TopicServiceImpl.findById() — combined with the HTTP Cache-Control: public, max-age=5 header set by the controller — to serve a cached response with stale custom fee data to victims who query the topic within the 5-second window after a fee schedule change, causing fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() uses topicRepository.findById(id.getId()) where getId() returns a long, and the JPA/Spring Data repository maps this to a SQL query 'SELECT * FROM topic WHERE id = ?', to perform a SQL timing side-channel attack that reveals whether specific topic IDs exist, enabling targeted fee manipulation attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() does not validate the shard component of the EntityId against the network's configured shard, allowing cross-shard topic ID lookups that return topics from a different shard's data in the database, exposing incorrect fee schedules that cause fund loss when victims submit messages to the wrong network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId encoding formula '(num & NUM_MASK) | (realm & REALM_MASK) << NUM_BITS | (shard & SHARD_MASK) << (REALM_BITS + NUM_BITS)' to find two topic IDs with different shard/realm/num values that produce the same 64-bit encoded long, causing topicRepository.findById() to return the wrong topic's data and exposing incorrect custom fees that lead to fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() is not annotated with @Transactional, causing the repository call to execute outside a transaction context, leading to inconsistent reads of the topic's custom fee data during concurrent updates, and causing a victim to receive wrong fee information that results in fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() propagates the raw EntityNotFoundException from the repository layer with the message 'Topic not found', leaking information about which topic IDs exist in the database, enabling an attacker to enumerate valid topic IDs and target high-value topics with custom fees for exploitation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() returns a Topic domain object that includes the topic's submit key, allowing the attacker to verify whether a topic requires a submit key, and then use that information to identify topics with no submit key (open topics) that have high custom fees, and craft social engineering attacks to trick victims into submitting messages to those topics, causing fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() does not verify that the Topic returned by topicRepository.findById(id.getId()) has an id field matching the requested id.getId(), allowing an ORM-level bug where a topic with a different primary key is returned due to second-level cache poisoning, causing incorrect fee data to be served and leading to fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId.of(long id) method's use of a static Caffeine cache to perform a cache-timing attack, measuring response times to determine whether a topic ID is cached (fast) or not (slow), thereby inferring which topic IDs are frequently accessed and targeting high-traffic fee-bearing topics for exploitation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() is called in TopicController without checking the topic's deletion status, causing the API to return fee data for a topic that has been deleted on the network but not yet reflected in the mirror node database, leading a victim to submit a fee-bearing message to a deleted topic and lose funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() does not enforce any access control on which topics can be queried, allowing an attacker to retrieve the custom fee schedule of a topic before it is publicly announced, and use that advance knowledge to front-run fee-bearing transactions for financial gain?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() does not validate the realm component of the EntityId, allowing a cross-realm topic ID lookup that returns a topic from a different realm's data, exposing incorrect custom fee schedules that cause fund loss when victims submit messages expecting the fees of a different realm's topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() does not log or audit topic lookups, allowing an attacker to silently enumerate all topic custom fee schedules without detection, and use that information to identify and exploit topics with high fees for financial gain?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() does not check whether the Topic's fee collector account is valid or active, causing a victim to submit a fee-bearing message to a topic whose fee collector account has been deleted, resulting in the fee being burned or lost rather than collected, causing direct fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() does not validate the consistency between the Topic's fee schedule and the entity's fee schedule returned by entityService.findById(), allowing a data inconsistency where the topic table and entity table have different fee data, causing the TopicMapper to produce a response with mixed fee information that leads to fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() does not check whether the Topic's custom fee denominating token exists or is active, causing a victim to submit a message expecting to pay fees in a specific token, only to have the transaction fail because the token is frozen or deleted, while still paying network fees, resulting in direct fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() does not validate the Topic's fee schedule effective timestamp, causing a victim to receive a fee schedule that has not yet taken effect or has already expired, leading to incorrect fee assumptions and fund loss when submitting messages?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() does not check whether the Topic's fee schedule has been superseded by a newer version in the database, causing a victim to receive an outdated fee schedule and submit a message expecting old fees but being charged new higher fees, resulting in direct fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() does not validate the Topic's fee schedule against the network's current consensus state, causing a victim to receive fee data that differs from what the network will actually charge, leading to direct fund loss when the transaction is executed?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() does not check whether the Topic's fee schedule includes fees denominated in HBAR vs. tokens, causing a victim to misinterpret the fee type and submit a message with insufficient HBAR, having the transaction fail while still paying network fees, resulting in direct fund loss?
